# Evidence Brief - Energy & climate (SDG 7, 13): Kenya

*Generated by CEDAR on 2026-06-20T14:52:41Z | Glass-box mode: every figure is cited below.*

## Key findings

- [+] Access to electricity rose from 19.2 to 76.2 % of population between 2010 and 2023 (+296.9%, +11.2%/yr). [1]
- [!] As of 2023, the value of 76.2 does not yet meet the benchmark of 100 (SDG 7.1: universal access to electricity by 2030); a gap of 23.8 % of population remains. [2]
- [-] Renewable energy share fell from 74 to 67.7 % of final energy between 2010 and 2021 (-8.5%, -0.8%/yr). [3]
- [-] CO2 emissions per capita rose from 0.32 to 0.38 t CO2e/capita between 2010 and 2023 (+18.8%, +1.3%/yr). [4]

## Data confidence & caveats

- **Access to electricity** - confidence: **High** (coverage 100%, latest 2023).
- **Renewable energy share** - confidence: **Medium** (coverage 100%, latest 2021).
    - Caveat: Latest available data point is 2021; figures may lag current conditions.
- **CO2 emissions per capita** - confidence: **High** (coverage 100%, latest 2023).

## Methods & reproducibility

All figures are pulled from authoritative APIs and computed deterministically (trend = first vs latest observation; CAGR = compound annual rate; gap = distance to published benchmark). No figure is model-generated. Re-run any number with the query URLs in the evidence ledger below.
